summary: Implemented the Stats Page (Tab 4) with all dashboard components: HeroCard (weekly volume + week-over-week change), FourGridSummary (4-card grid with sessions/duration/PRs), WeeklyVolumeChart (8-week bar chart), PRList (top 4 exercises with Epley 1RM), TrainingHeatmap (28-day frequency grid with intensity shading), and EmptyStatsState. Created StatsService for computing all statistics from WorkoutSession/WorkoutSet/PersonalRecord/Exercise repos.
keyDecisions: Used pure computation StatsService with repo injection pattern (same as other services like calendar-computer, pr-tracker)
Used simple View-based bar chart instead of victory-native BarChart to avoid native dependency issues in testing
Heatmap uses intensity thresholds: rest=0.1 (<1 volume), light=0.4 (<2000), moderate=0.6-0.8 (<20000), heavy=0.9+ (>=20000)
FourGridSummary uses Dimensions-based card width calculation instead of CSS calc() (not supported in RN)
Stats screen uses placeholder state pattern - will be wired to actual data via store/hook in future tasks
Exported formatting functions (formatVolume, formatChangePct, formatWeight, getIntensityColor, formatCompact) for testability

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Hero card: weekly volume with week-over-week change percentage (green up / red down)
Four-grid summary cards: weekly sessions (current/target), monthly sessions (consecutive weeks), weekly total duration (hours), monthly new PRs
Weekly volume bar chart: last 8 weeks, current week highlighted in accent, last week green border
PR list: top 4 exercises with estimated 1RM and date, link to history PR panel
Training frequency heatmap: 28-day grid, intensity shading (rest=0.1, light=0.4-0.6, moderate=0.7-0.8, heavy=0.9+)
Planned but incomplete dates: blue border on heatmap
Empty state: prompt for first training
Week-over-week formula: thisWeekVolume / lastWeekVolume - 1; no data shows --

notes: Stats screen currently shows empty state placeholder. Database integration will be wired up when the store/hook layer is created. The bar chart is implemented with plain Views rather than victory-native for testability and simplicity.
